summary: Phase 5 Fulfillment Architecture backend tested end-to-end. 12/13 tests passed (1 skipped: live checkout/Stripe). Migration durability, ProductUpdate (reorder_point + download_url), low-stock admin endpoint (auth/forbidden/shape/sort/forced inclusion), and order has_warehouse/has_dropship flags (legacy backfill + all-warehouse synthetic) all verified.

minor.issue: Returns 5xx (Stripe likely unconfigured in this pod). Accepted per task brief — synthetic order tests cover Phase 5D flag logic.

critical_code_review_comments: migrate_fulfillment.py is non-idempotent (re-randomises split on every run) — script docstring already warns of this; acceptable per user choice but consider adding an --idempotent flag for production safety.
admin_low_stock uses $expr with $ifNull on reorder_point — good defensive handling for legacy docs missing the field; result includes single image (first of images[]) which matches spec.

Order model defaults has_warehouse/has_dropship to False; legacy backfill in admin_list_orders only triggers when keys are missing — fine for the synthetic legacy case; new orders correctly set flags in commerce_routes.py:127-128.
seed_data_creation: Inserted 2 synthetic orders (AM-TEST-*) and removed them after assertions. Forced a warehouse product into stock=2 / reorder_point=10 to validate low-stock results (state left as-is to demo the feature).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_phase5_fulfillment.py contains reusable suite. STRIPE_API_KEY is missing in this pod so /api/checkout/session returns 5xx — synthetic order insertion via PyMongo is the recommended path to validate Phase 5D flags. One warehouse product was intentionally left at stock=2 reorder_point=10 for the low-stock card demo.
